senses.definition: Played or sung at a moderately slow, walking speed.
examples: The second movement is andante, creating a calm mood.
Please play this section andante rather than too fast.
The piece begins with an andante passage for strings.

senses.definition: At a moderately slow, walking speed.
examples: The choir sings andante in the opening bars.
The pianist plays andante, giving the melody space to breathe.
The violins should move andante here for a gentle feeling.

senses.definition: A piece of music or part of a piece that is played at a moderately slow tempo.
examples: The orchestra performed a beautiful andante from the symphony.
The sonata includes a lyrical andante in the middle.
He wrote an andante for solo violin and piano.

senses.definition: Plural of andante: musical pieces or sections played at a moderately slow tempo.
examples: The concert program features two andantes and a finale.
The composer is known for his lyrical andantes.
These andantes are often performed as standalone pieces.

senses.definition: Slightly slower or lighter than andante in musical tempo.
examples: The score indicates an andantini section after the introduction.
She plays the andantini passage with great sensitivity.
The movement moves from allegro to andantini and back again.

senses.definition: At a tempo slightly faster or sometimes slightly slower than andante, depending on the tradition.
examples: The third movement is marked andantino, giving it a light character.
Please keep the andantino section flowing and not too slow.
This andantino piece is often played as an encore.

senses.definition: A piece of music or movement played at an andantino tempo.
examples: The pianist chose an andantino by Chopin for the recital.
This andantino is one of the composer’s most loved works.
He wrote an expressive andantino for string quartet.

senses.definition: Plural of andantino: musical pieces or movements with an andantino tempo.
examples: The album includes three andantinos and a finale.
These andantinos show the composer’s lyrical style.
He is famous for his delicate andantinos for piano.

senses.definition: An Indigenous people from the southern highlands of Colombia.
examples: Anthropologists have studied the culture of the Andaqui.
The Andaqui lived in the Upper Caquetá River region.
Some Andaqui communities still exist in rural Colombia.

senses.definition: The traditional language of the Andaqui people, now considered extinct or nearly extinct.
examples: Very few records of the Andaqui language survive.
Linguists debate how to classify Andaqui.
The Andaqui language is no longer actively spoken.

senses.definition: Relating to the Andaqui people or their language.
examples: Researchers wrote about Andaquian traditions and beliefs.
The museum hosts an exhibit on Andaquian artifacts.
She specializes in Andaquian cultural history.

senses.definition: A rare family name or place name, not commonly used in general English.
examples: The village of Andarko appears on some old maps.
She traced her family name back to Andarko in the 19th century.
Very little is written about Andarko in English sources.

senses.definition: An older name used for certain Indigenous peoples of North America, especially the Susquehannock.
examples: Early European maps sometimes label the region as Andaste.
The name Andaste appears in several colonial records.
Some historians still refer to the group as Andaste in older texts.

senses.definition: A rare or archaic word, sometimes used regionally as a form of 'and' in dialect writing.
examples: In the old text, 'ande' is used instead of 'and'.
The poem keeps the dialect spellings, including 'ande' for 'and'.
Readers may find forms like 'ande' in Middle English manuscripts.

senses.definition: Relating to the Andes mountains or the countries along them, such as Peru, Bolivia, and Chile.
examples: The Andean region has many active volcanoes.
She studies Andean culture and music.
The village lies in an Andean valley.

senses.definition: Relating to the Indigenous peoples, languages, or traditions of the Andes.
examples: Andean textiles are famous around the world.
She researches Andean languages in rural communities.
Andean traditions often mix Indigenous and Christian beliefs.

senses.definition: A male given name of Germanic or Scandinavian origin.
examples: Anders gave a talk on climate change.
She named her son Anders after his grandfather.
Anders moved from Sweden to Canada in his twenties.

senses.definition: A family name found in several European and English-speaking countries.
examples: Dr. Anders will see you now.
The Anders family owns the restaurant on the corner.
Her last name is Anders, not Anderson.

senses.definition: A common family name in English-speaking and Scandinavian countries.
examples: Ms. Anderson teaches chemistry at the local high school.
The Anderson family moved into the house next door.
He introduced himself simply as Anderson.

senses.definition: Used as a place name for cities or towns in some English-speaking countries.
examples: They drove from Anderson to the state capital.
Anderson is a small city with a growing tech industry.
She grew up in Anderson and later moved abroad.

senses.definition: A very rare or obsolete word, not used in modern general English.
examples: The term 'anderun' appears only in a few old dictionaries.
Most modern readers will not recognize the word 'anderun'.
He noted 'anderun' as an obsolete form in his glossary.

senses.definition: A long mountain range along the western coast of South America.
examples: The Andes run through several South American countries.
Many travelers visit the Andes to go trekking.
The Andes include some of the highest peaks in the world.

senses.definition: Made of or relating to andesite rock.
examples: The volcano erupted andesic lava during the last event.
Geologists mapped large andesic formations in the region.
The island is mainly built from andesic rocks.

senses.definition: A pale-colored mineral of the feldspar group, often found in volcanic rocks.
examples: The sample contained crystals of andesine.
Andesine is a common component of some volcanic rocks.
The gemstone dealer showed us a ring set with red andesine.

senses.definition: Plural of andesine: crystals or pieces of the mineral andesine.
examples: The rock contains several visible andesines.
These andesines show a strong red color.
The gem collection includes rare andesines.

senses.definition: A rare mineral related to andesite rocks, mentioned mainly in specialist texts.
examples: The geologist noted traces of andesinite in the sample.
Andesinite is described only briefly in the mineral guide.
Few collections include andesinite due to its rarity.

senses.definition: A grey or dark volcanic rock with a mineral content between basalt and rhyolite.
examples: The island is mostly made of andesite and basalt.
Andesite is common in volcanic mountain belts like the Andes.
They collected pieces of andesite from the lava flow.

senses.definition: Plural of andesite: pieces or types of the volcanic rock andesite.
examples: The range is built from basalts and andesites.
These andesites formed during different eruptions.
Geologists compared the chemistry of several andesites.

senses.definition: Made of or resembling andesite, especially in the composition of volcanic rocks or lava.
examples: The volcano mainly produces andesitic lava.
Andesitic rocks dominate this mountain belt.
They studied the andesitic magma beneath the volcano.

senses.definition: A nonstandard or misspelled form of 'andesite', sometimes seen in older or informal texts.
examples: The report used the spelling 'andesyte' for the rock type.
Modern texts usually correct 'andesyte' to 'andesite'.
Students should avoid the form 'andesyte' in formal writing.

senses.definition: Plural of the nonstandard form 'andesyte', corresponding to 'andesites'.
examples: Only a few old papers refer to 'andesytes'.
Nowadays geologists write 'andesites' instead of 'andesytes'.
The teacher corrected 'andesytes' to 'andesites' in the report.

senses.definition: A rare or region-specific term not used in standard contemporary English.
examples: The dictionary lists 'andevo' as an uncommon regional word.
Most speakers of English will never encounter 'andevo'.
He marked 'andevo' as obscure in his notes.

senses.definition: Short for Andhra Pradesh, a state in southeastern India.
examples: He grew up in Andhra and later moved to Delhi.
Andhra is known for its spicy cuisine.
Many people from Andhra work in the tech industry.

senses.definition: A given name, often used as a short form of Andrew or Andrea.
examples: Andi started a new job last month.
I met Andi at the conference in London.
Her friends call her Andi instead of Andrea.

senses.definition: A rare place or family name found in some languages but not common in general English use.
examples: The village of Andia is mentioned in the travel guide.
Her surname is Andia, which comes from her grandparents.
Very few English speakers are familiar with the name Andia.

senses.definition: A rare or nonstandard form sometimes used instead of 'Andean'.
examples: The old article refers to 'Andian' traditions in the mountains.
Most modern texts prefer 'Andean' over 'Andian'.
She corrected 'Andian' to 'Andean' in the manuscript.

senses.definition: Relating to the Andes mountains or their natural environment; another form of 'Andean' used mainly in technical or older texts.
examples: The study focused on andine flora and fauna.
She wrote a paper on andine climate patterns.
Andine glaciers are retreating due to global warming.

senses.definition: A place name used for towns or districts in some Asian countries.
examples: He was born in Anding, a small rural town.
The road from Anding to the capital was recently improved.
They plan to visit Anding during their trip.

senses.definition: A tropical tree of the genus Andira, found mainly in Central and South America.
examples: The forest contains several large andira trees.
Andira is known locally for its hard wood.
Some species of andira are used in traditional medicine.

senses.definition: An obscure or obsolete term not used in modern standard English.
examples: The glossary lists 'andirin' without a clear explanation.
He found the word 'andirin' only in one historical source.
Teachers rarely, if ever, discuss words like 'andirin'.

senses.definition: A chemical compound obtained from seeds of trees in the genus Andira, mentioned mainly in older scientific literature.
examples: The study described the extraction of andirine from the seeds.
Andirine was once tested for medicinal uses.
Modern research rarely refers to andirine directly.

senses.definition: A tropical tree native to the Amazon region, whose seeds are used to make a medicinal oil.
examples: The villagers harvest andiroba for its valuable oil.
Andiroba grows well in flooded forests.
They use andiroba oil to treat insect bites.

senses.definition: One of a pair of metal supports in a fireplace that hold up the logs.
examples: The logs rested on the iron andiron by the hearth.
She polished the brass andiron before lighting the fire.
The old house still had its original andiron in the fireplace.
collocations: set the logs on the andiron

senses.definition: A pair of metal supports used in a fireplace to hold logs.
examples: She placed the wood carefully on the andirons.
The antique andirons added charm to the old fireplace.
They replaced the rusty andirons with a new set.

senses.definition: An Indigenous people of Colombia, also called Andoque, living near the Amazon and Putumayo rivers.
examples: The Andoke maintain many of their traditional customs.
Anthropologists have documented the beliefs of the Andoke.
Only a small number of Andoke still speak the language.

senses.definition: Used to show that one or both of two things may happen or be true.
examples: The course is open to students and/or staff.
You may send questions by email and/or letter.
Payment can be made in cash and/or by card.

senses.definition: A dark-colored mineral containing silver and lead, found in some ore deposits.
examples: The mine produces small amounts of andorite.
Andorite often occurs together with other silver minerals.
They identified the mineral as andorite under the microscope.

senses.definition: Another name for the andiroba tree, used in some regions.
examples: Locals call the tree andoroba and use its oil for medicine.
The forest contains many tall andoroba trees.
He studied how andoroba oil is produced.

senses.definition: A regional or variant name for the andiroba tree in some areas.
examples: The healer uses oil from the andorobo in his treatments.
Andorobo grows well in wet tropical forests.
She explained how andorobo is important to the local economy.

senses.definition: A small country in the Pyrenees mountains between France and Spain.
examples: Andorra is famous for its ski resorts.
They drove through Andorra on their way to Spain.
Andorra has its own government and official language.

senses.definition: Relating to Andorra, its people, or its culture.
examples: Andorran laws are influenced by both France and Spain.
She enjoys Andorran food when she goes skiing.
Andorran shops attract many tourists.

senses.definition: A person from Andorra.
examples: He is an Andorran who works in tourism.
Many Andorrans speak several languages.
An Andorran guided us around the town.

senses.definition: A spicy smoked sausage, originally from France and now also common in Cajun cooking.
examples: The soup was flavored with slices of andouille.
She bought some andouille at the market for the gumbo.
Andouille adds a rich, smoky taste to many dishes.

senses.definition: Plural of andouille: several sausages of that type.
examples: The butcher offered fresh andouilles this morning.
She fried the andouilles before adding them to the stew.
These andouilles are milder than the ones we had in France.

senses.definition: A small French sausage similar to andouille, often made with pork intestines.
examples: He ordered an andouillet at the traditional restaurant.
Andouillet has a strong flavor that some people love.
The menu listed andouillet with mustard sauce.

senses.definition: A traditional French sausage made from pork intestines and stomach, known for its strong smell and taste.
examples: He tried andouillette for the first time in Lyon.
Andouillette is often served with fries and mustard.
Not everyone enjoys the strong aroma of andouillette.

senses.definition: Plural of andouillette: several sausages of that type.
examples: The market stall sold freshly grilled andouillettes.
He ordered two andouillettes with a salad.
These andouillettes are a speciality of the region.

senses.definition: A type of garnet mineral that can be green, yellow, or brown and is sometimes used as a gemstone.
examples: The jeweler showed us a ring set with green andradite.
Andradite is valued by collectors for its bright colors.
They found crystals of andradite in the rock sample.
